text.title: Graph RAG in Production Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the graph-rag-production methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every incoming query MUST first hit a router that classifies it as GLOBAL / LOCAL / COMMUNITY / NONE, then dispatches to the matching retrieval path.
rule.rationale: Running the wrong retrieval path (e.g. global on a local question) wastes 10x cost AND degrades quality; routing is the cheapest precision lever.

rule.statement: Community summaries served from cache keyed by (community_id, index_version); cache miss triggers regeneration AND emits metric.
rule.rationale: Without versioned cache keys, an index re-build silently serves stale summaries until eviction.

rule.statement: Document inserts/deletes MUST go through a delta event stream that updates entities + edges + affected communities; no in-place mutation of the live graph.
rule.rationale: Live mutation races against in-flight retrievals; the delta stream gives an atomic apply point.

rule.statement: Each query type MUST carry a max-cost cap (USD); router rejects with HTTP 429 above cap and emits alert.
rule.rationale: One adversarial GLOBAL query can cost 100x a normal LOCAL one; without a cap, a single attacker blows the budget.

rule.statement: If traffic < 100 queries/day OR index is read-only static, skip production patterns and run the simpler retrieval methodology directly.
rule.rationale: Production overhead (router, cache, delta stream) only amortizes above a traffic floor.
